title: 1.1: Reports how data transfers from C structures to Python kittens (e.g., Handler class callbacks, _initialize method parameters)
1.2: Identifies the callback architecture for event delivery (e.g., on_key_event, on_mouse_event, on_clipboard_response methods)
1.3: States clipboard data handling for large payloads (e.g., OSC 52 processing, chunking behavior with continuation flags)
1.4: Reports memory allocation mechanisms crossing Python/C boundary (e.g., PyMem_RawMalloc, PyMem_RawRealloc usage)
1.5: Identifies mutex, synchronization mechanisms protecting shared data (e.g., screen_mutex macro, pthread_mutex usage)
1.7: Reports timing mechanisms for event correlation (e.g., monotonic function, ButtonEvent timestamp tracking)
1.8: Identifies bounded queue, buffer mechanisms preventing unbounded growth (e.g., button event deque with size limit of 5)
1.9: States reference counting for object ownership (e.g., INCREF_CHILD, DECREF_CHILD macros, Py_CLEAR usage)
